events.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
events.description: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds

events.title: Extreme heat pushing global food systems to the brink, UN agencies warn
events.description: Extreme heat is pushing global food and farming systems to the brink, threatening the livelihoods of over a billion people as rising temperatures and more frequent heatwaves redefine how food is produced worldwide, a new UN report warns.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Extreme heat pushing global food systems to the brink, UN agencies warn

events.title: Mass trial of MS-13 gang at CECOT prison
events.description: El Salvador has launched a mass trial of 486 alleged MS-13 members accused by prosecutors of thousands of murders and other crimes, as President Nayib Bukele continues his sweeping crackdown under a state of emergency that has sharply reduced crime, though human rights groups warn the measures—introduced in 2022—have led to serious abuses, including violations of due process, reports of torture, and more than 500 deaths in prison, as reported by Caroline Baum.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mass trial of MS-13 gang at CECOT prison

events.title: Trump orders navy to shoot Iranian boats
events.description: The standoff between the United States and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz appears to be edging closer to renewed conflict than a peace deal, with President Donald Trump ordering the Navy to target Iranian boats laying mines and Israel signaling it is ready to resume bombing once it receives the go-ahead from Washington, as Oliver Farry from our international affairs desk explains.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Trump orders navy to shoot Iranian boats

events.title: A mass trial in El Salvador and other top photos from Latin America and the Caribbean
events.description: April 17–23, 2026 Alleged gang members were put on mass trial at the Terrorist Confinement Center, or CECOT, in Tecoluca, El Salvador. A gunman fired at tourists from atop one of Mexico's Teotihuacan pyramids, killing a Canadian person and wounded others. ___ This gallery was curated by photographer Dolores Ochoa, based in Quito, Ecuador. ___ AP photography: https://apnews.com/photography Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/apnews
events.ticker_label: 💥 A mass trial in El Salvador and other top photos from Latin America and the Caribbean

events.title: Middle East war: After oil and gas, concerns grow over minerals crunch

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: After oil and gas, concerns grow over minerals crunch

events.title: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
events.description: ICC judges say there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands The former president of the Philippines, Rodrigo Duterte , will face trial at the international criminal court (ICC) after judges unanimously confirmed charges of crimes against humanity over his “war on drugs”. Pre-trial judges concluded on Thursday that there were substantial grounds to believe Duterte was responsible for the crimes against humanity of murder and attempted murder in relation to anti-drugs crackdowns that led to the killing of thousands of people. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Regional war threatens Syria’s fragile transition
events.description: The Security Council is meeting now at a critical juncture for Syria’s fragile transition. While the Iran-US ceasefire has temporarily eased regional spillover from Lebanon and elsewhere, conditions remain volatile in Syria. The reported US military withdrawal now completed, marks a milestone, yet the chamber faces a country grappling with deep internal fissures and external pressures. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users should click here.

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Update on Haiti as humanitarian crisis continues alongside gang violence
events.description: The Security Council is addressing the deepening crisis in Haiti on Thursday, centered on the Secretary-General’s latest report which highlights a security landscape of both intensified enforcement and rising civilian risk. While operations including by the UN-backed Gang Suppression Force (GSF) between December and February resulted in the deaths of 1,343 suspected gang members, the humanitarian cost remains staggering. The UN Special Representative told ambassadors elections are the “only legitimate path” back to political stability, while his counterpart with the GSF said its success depends on “effective coordination”. Follow full meetings coverage below.
events.ticker_label: 💥 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Update on Haiti as humanitarian crisis continues alongside gang violence

events.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
events.description: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers. Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022, including a weekend that was El Salvador’s bloodiest since its civil war. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang

events.title: PM speaks to Vietnamese president on supply chains – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Allegra Spender says 25% gas export tax would help fix ‘faulty’ system Independent MP Allegra Spender said a 25% tax on gas exports would help rectify what she sees as “faulty” taxation arrangements that have seen an Australian resource sent overseas with minimal benefit to the country. The gas industry is a very profitable industry and pays income tax. And every company in Australia, frankly, should pay income tax on its profits and should pay the proper rate. But the gas companies are different because they also sell an Australian resource which they extract, which we can’t get back once it is sold. I think Australians rightly believe they should share more of that revenue. We’re back here again and they should fix it. We are lucky to be an energy exporter at a time where the world needs energy. We are a great partner in this. But it is a reasonable thing for Australians to get a fair return on that. And, at the moment, we just aren’t. We can’t and I think we need to be really honest about that. If there’s going to be changes to the NDIS – and I’m not a state leader who’s knocking the federal government’s right and probably responsibility to reform the NDIS, it’s cost too much money – but we have to be really frank with people. We can’t offer at the state level the kinds of services that are being rolled out at the NDIS. Continue reading...

events.title: Hormuz standoff chokes 20% of oil supply
events.description: In the Strait of Hormuz, the United States and Iran are locked in a struggle for influence over one of the world’s most strategic waterways, with Washington emphasizing ship seizures to demonstrate its ability to disrupt maritime traffic, while a parallel on-the-water confrontation unfolds over demining and securing the key global oil route, amid a broader battle of messaging and control, as reported by Liza Kaminov and France 2.

events.title: Number of billionaires globally could reach 4,000 in next five years
events.description: There are now 3,110 billionaires but analysis shows ‘deep structural acceleration’ in wealth creation around world The number of billionaires in the world could reach nearly 4,000 by 2031, figures suggest, as the super-rich accumulate wealth at an accelerating rate. There are now 3,110 billionaires globally, according to analysis by the estate agent Knight Frank. This is forecast to rise by 25% over the next five years, taking the total to 3,915. Continue reading...

events.title: Eye on Africa: Tanzania poll violence report

The student, who the Guardian has chosen not to name, lost contact with his friends in Sydney after returning to China in December 2024. Continue reading...

events.title: Embassy encourages Australia-based Iranians to volunteer to help war effort

As the war approaches the two-month mark, the embassy’s website and Telegram channel this week posted a message that instructed people on how to register for a voluntary drive to fight for their country , known as the “Janfada” – or “sacrificing life” – program. events.title: Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax
events.description: David Pocock says prime minister – who is trying to shore up fuel supplies – is parroting industry talking points Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor is poised to reject a growing push for a new 25% tax on gas exports in next month’s budget, prompting David Pocock to accuse the government of “caving in” to the gas industry. It’s understood the government has elected not to pursue a new tax on gas exports in the budget , prompted in part by the global oil crisis and Anthony Albanese’s diplomatic efforts in shoring up fuel supply from Asian allies by pledging reliable access to liquefied natural gas. events.title: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex
events.description: Karex produces more than 5 billion condoms annually and is a supplier to leading brands like Durex and Trojan, as well as the NHS The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said. Karex is also seeing a surge in condom demand as rising freight costs and shipping delays have left many of its customers with lower stockpiles than usual, CEO Goh Miah Kiat told Reuters in an interview on Tuesday. events.title: Ben Roberts-Smith to attend first Anzac Day service since war crime charges
events.description: Former soldier says he will ‘pay my respects’ at commemoration in Queensland where he has been living since being released on bail Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Ben Roberts-Smith will attend an Anzac Day service in Queensland on Saturday morning, describing the day as “sacred” to him, the first commemoration since he was criminally charged. Roberts-Smith, the recipient of the Victoria Cross and once one of Australia’s most lionised soldiers, faces five charges of the war crime of murder, allegedly committed during his service with the SAS in Afghanistan between 2009 and 2012. Continue reading...

events.title: Pilot’s selfie led to mid-air collision in F-15K fighter jet, says South Korea’s air force
events.description: One pilot ordered to repay some of the $600,000 of damage caused by collision in 2021 South Korea’s air force has apologised for a 2021 mid-air collision involving two fighter jets, a day after auditors said pilots were taking selfies and filming during the flight and held them responsible for the accident. “We sincerely apologise to the public for the concern caused by the accident that occurred in 2021,” an air force spokesperson said in a press briefing. The spokesperson said one of the pilots involved had been suspended from flying duties, received severe disciplinary action and has since left the military. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Pilot’s selfie led to mid-air collision in F-15K fighter jet, says South Korea’s air force

events.title: Strait of Hormuz: Iran, US seize ships

events.title: US soldier involved in Maduro raid charged over alleged bets on capture
events.description: Gannon Ken Van Dyke, who allegedly made more than $400,000 on Polymarket, could face up to 60 years in prison A US soldier who played a role in the January capture of Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro is now in custody after allegedly cashing in over $400,000 on wagers about the politician’s removal from office, federal authorities announced on Thursday. Prosecutors say beginning in early December the soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, was involved in planning for the military operation to capture and depose Maduro. Continue reading...

events.title: St Vincent and Grenadines government pauses constitutional amendment bills after public backlash
events.description: Bills are seeking to change section that opposition says makes Godwin Friday, a dual citizen, ineligible to be PM The St Vincent and the Grenadines government has delayed a controversial effort to amend a section of the country’s constitution that the opposition says renders the prime minister ineligible for his position in parliament. Two bills, among six listed for the parliament session on Tuesday this week, were aimed at clarifying a section of the 1979 constitution governing the citizenship eligibility of members of parliament. Continue reading...

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks

events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. Continue reading...

events.title: Charlize Theron joins chorus of disapproval over Timothée Chalamet’s ballet comments
events.description: The former ballet dancer said Chalamet’s comments were ‘reckless’ in an interview with the New York Times in which she also discussed her violent childhood Actor and former ballet dancer Charlize Theron has joined the chorus of disapproval aimed at Timothée Chalamet over his remarks that appeared to disrespect performers of ballet and opera . In an interview with the New York Times , Theron said: “Oh, boy, I hope I run into him one day,” adding: “That was a very reckless comment on two art forms that we need to lift up constantly because, yes, they do have a hard time. But in 10 years, AI is going to be able to do Timothée’s job, but it will not be able to replace a person on a stage dancing live.” Continue reading...

events.title: How TikTok is driving American expats to Southeast Asia
events.description: Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.

events.title: Natural molecule could provide Ozempic, Wegovy alternative
events.description: Discovered using AI, a naturally occurring molecule appears to act directly in the brain’s appetite center. Scientists hope it could refine treatments for obesity, offering fewer side effects for some patients.

events.title: Nepal: Mount Everest ice block obstructs large group of spring climbers
events.description: A chunk of ice suspended above the Khumbu Icefall near Base Camp on Mount Everest means it's not safe either for climbers or the specialist "icefall doctors" who typically clear away smaller obstructions.

events.title: Thai opposition figures face trial over attempt to change royal defamation law
events.description: Forty-four lawmakers could be banned from office for seeking to change Thailand's strict lese-majeste law, which criminalizes criticism of the monarchy. Critics say it has been used to silence opponents.

events.title: China's DeepSeek launches preview of new AI model
events.description: DeepSeek, the startup behind a low-cost chatbot that sent shockwaves through the AI community last year, has released its highly anticipated V4 model.

events.title: Meta, Microsoft purge jobs amid AI build-up
events.description: Facebook's parent company said it would slash 10% of its workforce, while Microsoft is offering an early retirement scheme. The cuts come as both tech giants make massive investments in AI.

events.title: US soldier allegedly made $400k on Polymarket betting on Maduro raid
events.description: Prosecutors say the soldier used classified information of the Maduro operation to place winning bets on Polymarket. The case comes amid other incidents of prediction market bets tied to US government actions.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US soldier allegedly made $400k on Polymarket betting on Maduro raid

events.title: UK: Health data volunteered by 500,000 people to Biobank charity listed for sale on Alibaba in China
events.description: The British government is investigating how health data volunteered to a charity for research purposes wound up for sale via three vendors on the Alibaba e-commerce site in China. One listing had 500,000 people's data.

events.title: After losing her son, healthy British woman will legally end her life in Swiss clinic
events.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, April 24: We look at reactions in the German, Russian and Kazakh press after an important oil pipeline from Russia is suspended to Germany. Russia says it's for "technical" reasons, but the German press say it is "political". Also: the press is divided over the case of a physically healthy, deeply grieving British woman who will die by euthanasia in a Swiss clinic this Friday. Finally, we look at the most surprising clauses of pre-nup agreements.

events.title: US-China AI race intensifies as DeepSeek releases 'reduced' cost model
events.description: China's DeepSeek on Friday released a new AI model with "drastically reduced" costs that it said was capable of processing extra-long texts to help it complete tasks. The company caused shockwaves last year after it revealed a reasoning model that upended assumptions of US dominance in the sector.

events.title: US soldier 'made hundreds of thousands of dollars betting on Maduro capture'
events.description: A US special forces soldier involved in the military operation that captured Nicolas Maduro has been charged with allegedly betting on the raid.

events.title: Prosecutors seek 30-year prison term for South Korea's Yoon
events.description: South Korean prosecutors are requesting a 30-year prison term for ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol over allegations he deliberately tried to escalate tensions with rival North Korea by ordering drone flights over Pyongyang
events.ticker_label: 💥 Prosecutors seek 30-year prison term for South Korea's Yoon

events.title: China to send giant pandas to Atlanta again
events.description: China is sending two giant pandas to Zoo Atlanta, the China Wildlife Conservation Association says

events.title: South Africa's police chief suspended over corruption allegations
events.description: South Africa's top police official, Fannie Masemola, has been suspended by the president after being charged with breaking finance laws

events.title: New bridge helps cement Lesotho as water lifeline for South Africa’s economic hub
events.description: A newly built bridge in Lesotho is set to nearly double its water exports to South Africa

events.title: EU pulls $2.4 million from Venice Biennale over Russia's return
events.description: The Venice Biennale has lost 2-million-euro ($2.4 million) in funding due to its decision to allow Russia to participate in the 61st contemporary art show

events.title: Iran’s exiled crown prince Reza Pahlavi splattered with red liquid in Berlin
events.description: Iran’s exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi was splattered with red liquid as he left a building after a news conference in Berlin

events.title: Britain and France sign a 3-year deal to curb small-boat channel crossings
events.description: Britain and France have signed a new multimillion-euro deal to reduce migrant crossings in the English Channel

events.title: Zambia says it has custody of ex-president's body in dispute with family over burial
events.description: A dispute over the remains of Zambia's former President Edgar Lungu continues as a court orders his body returned to his family

events.title: Time to apply the brakes to runaway AI, says pioneer
events.description: If AI is “a very fast car with no steering wheel” then regulation must provide one, insists Nobel laureate and Artificial Intelligence pioneer Geoffrey Hinton, the visionary scientist widely known as the “godfather” of the self-learning tech.

events.title: Economic collapse pushes highly educated Gazans into the ‘survival economy’
events.description: Young Palestinians in Gaza with university-level educations are setting aside dreams of putting their hard-won skills into practice and doing whatever they can to survive.

events.title: Zodiac Killer may be tied to Black Dahlia case after ‘code cracked,’ new suspect emerges
events.description: An independent investigator claims he decoded the Zodiac Killer's cipher and found evidence allegedly connecting it to the 1947 Black Dahlia murder.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Zodiac Killer may be tied to Black Dahlia case after ‘code cracked,’ new suspect emerges

events.title: Elite school teacher known as 'Mr Wonderful' accused of heinous crimes against students
events.description: A former teacher who was nicknamed "Mr. Wonderful" has been charged with three counts of rape involving two former students who allege years of abuse and grooming.

events.title: Army soldier involved in Maduro capture arrested for allegedly using classified intel to win $410K in bets
events.description: A soldier involved in Nicolás Maduro's capture allegedly placed insider bets on Polymarket using classified information, winning nearly $410,000.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Army soldier involved in Maduro capture arrested for allegedly using classified intel to win $410K in bets

events.title: Decades-old cold case breakthrough leads to arrest in execution-style killings of married couple before child
events.description: A Florida man is accused of gunning down a couple execution-style in front of their 3-year-old in a 1993 Bronx cold case that haunted a family for decades.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Decades-old cold case breakthrough leads to arrest in execution-style killings of married couple before child

events.title: Sex offender accused of 6 sex assaults in one day as crime surge rocks affluent valley
events.description: Registered sex offender Damian Robinson accused of a string of daytime sexual assaults in Los Angeles has turned himself in, authorities said.

events.title: 'Hero' father drowns saving his 12-year-old son from rip current at popular beach destination, family says
events.description: Jason DeGray died a hero after jumping into a deadly rip current at Cocoa Beach to save his son, his family said. A stranger who tried to help also lost her life.
events.ticker_label: 💥 'Hero' father drowns saving his 12-year-old son from rip current at popular beach destination, family says

events.title: UFO writer, paranormal YouTuber dies in suicide at home after mental health call: police
events.description: David Wilcock, a paranormal content creator and UFO researcher with hundreds of thousands of followers, died in an apparent suicide at age 53.
events.ticker_label: 💥 UFO writer, paranormal YouTuber dies in suicide at home after mental health call: police

events.title: DNA testing in Nancy Guthrie case could take months but may be fast-tracked, expert says
events.description: A genetic genealogist predicts DNA results in the Nancy Guthrie disappearance could take months after the FBI received a hair sample from a private lab.

events.title: US economic chokehold on Iran reaches peak leverage as collapse risks emerge
events.description: U.S. economic pressure on Iran has reached historic levels, but inconsistent enforcement limits full impact of sanctions, a former Treasury expert says.

events.title: Iran escalates Hormuz 'tit-for-tat,' seizes ship tied to billionaire close to Trump, Macron
events.description: Analysts say the IRGC seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz in a tit-for-tat move after a U.S. naval blockade and seizure of an Iranian ship.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Iran escalates Hormuz 'tit-for-tat,' seizes ship tied to billionaire close to Trump, Macron

events.title: Ex-Philippine president Duterte to face trial on crimes against humanity charges
events.description: The ICC confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte for allegedly overseeing deadly drug crackdowns.

events.title: Trump envoy to Turkey doubles down after backlash, pushes ‘peace through strength’ policy
events.description: U.S. Ambassador to Turkey and Syria envoy Tom Barrack defends controversial remarks on Hezbollah, Turkey’s F-35 access and Middle East governance as pragmatic realism, not policy shifts.
